comments: From a collection of 274 tapes mixing John Peel shows, Friday Rock Shows and Alan Freeman Saturday Shows from 1978 to 1983. The Derby Box was acquired by the John Peel Yahoo Group.

sessions.details: recorded 1976-06-10 for John Peel (Session #2). First TX 1976-06-25
recorded 1978-07-24 for John Peel (Session #2). First TX 1978-07-31

transcript.text: On 275 and 285 and Stereo VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Hi there, this is Tommy Vance. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show.
Tonight we've got sessions by the Frankie Miller Band, recorded way back in 1976,
and one by magazine from last year, featuring the voice of Howard DeVoto.
We've got classic tracks by The Stones, King Crimson, Bob Dylan, Leonard Skinner, Deep Purple, and also Montrose.
There are new cuts by Red Noise, Kevin Coyne, Cheap Trick, Aerosmith,
a never-before-heard version of a song that was put together by Deb Edmonds and Rockpile,
and a Friday Night Connection that could play havoc with your speaker cones
and also your cranial nerve ends.
